title: NASA Sends Two Spacecraft to Learn About Mars' Missing Air
summary: Mars used to be warm and wet with lots of air, but now it is cold and dry with very little air. Scientists think the solar wind, which is a stream of particles from the Sun, blew away Mars' atmosphere over billions of years. NASA launched two spacecraft called ESCAPADE on November 13, 2025, to study how this happened. These twin spacecraft will work together around Mars to watch how the solar wind interacts with the planet's magnetic field, helping scientists understand why Mars lost its air. This mission will also help protect future astronauts who might visit Mars by learning about space weather. The spacecraft started their instruments on February 25 and will collect data as they travel to Mars and once they arrive.

keywords.explanation: a stream of charged particles that comes from the Sun
the layer of air around a planet
machines sent into space to explore
an invisible force around a planet that can protect it

background_read: Mars is a planet in our solar system that scientists think was once like Earth, with water and air, but it changed over time. NASA is a space agency that sends missions to explore planets and learn about space. The solar wind is made of tiny particles from the Sun that can affect planets. Spacecraft are vehicles that travel in space to collect information. This mission helps us understand how planets lose their air and how to keep astronauts safe in space.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Launching twin spacecraft to study how Mars lost its atmosphere
WHERE: Around Mars and in space
WHY: To understand Mars' past and protect future astronauts

perspectives.description: Using technology to learn about Mars' history and changes
